franchisor_address: 7930 Jones Branch Drive, Suite 1100, McLean, Virginia 22102
predecessor: Hilton Franchise LLC (Oct 2007–Mar 2015); Hilton Inns, Inc. (Jul 1962–Oct 2007)

food_beverage_fee.amount: 3% of Gross Food and Beverage Revenue

program_fee.amount: 4% of GRR (max current + 1% GRR over term)

investment.excludes: Real property, market studies, insurance, interest, conversion/COO/re-licensing improvements

territory.protected_area: Not standard; negotiable Restricted Area Provision with carve-outs and time-limited Restrictive Period
territory.restricted_area_exclusions: Existing hotels, other Hilton brands, gaming, vacation ownership, acquisitions of 4+ hotels, Signia by Hilton
territory.conflict_resolution: None — entirely within business judgment of Hilton

term.renewal_terms: No renewal. Re-licensing at franchisor's sole discretion; may require materially different terms and PIP compliance

noncompete.during_term: May not own/be licensor or franchisor of competing brand. May own minority interest, be franchisee of competitor, or manage competitor property.

termination.interim_remedies: Suspension from Reservation Service, directory removal, system disablement, revocation of financial accommodations
termination.liquidated_damages_post_opening_pre_2yr: Greater of Hotel or System avg monthly royalty × 60
termination.liquidated_damages_last_60mo: Hotel avg monthly royalty × months remaining

transfer.permitted_transfers: Public equity, <50% private equity, institutional funds — some without notice, others with 60-day notice + $5,500 fee
transfer.coo_transfer: $150,000 fee, 60-day notice, current form FA, PIP compliance, transferee must meet current standards
transfer.right_of_first_offer: 30 days on unsolicited bona fide offers

dispute_resolution.forum: US District Court, Eastern District of Virginia (Alexandria, VA); or Fairfax County VA / New York NY (state court)

item20.gag_clause_text: During the last 3 fiscal years, some current and former franchisees have signed provisions restricting their ability to speak about their experience with us.

item21.debt_guarantee: All assets and franchise contracts pledged as collateral for Hilton's debt obligations (maturing 2025–2033)
item21.tax_status: Disregarded entity for US income tax purposes

litigation.pending_summary: 5 antitrust class actions related to revenue management software (IDeaS/STR/Amadeus) and alleged price-fixing through shared data
litigation.concluded_summary: Texas AG ($2.1M settlement, mandatory guest fees), Nebraska AG ($300K), Portland Hotel Ownership (dismissed with mutual releases), San Pedro Inn/Hampton (settled, franchise terminated)

training.brand_conference_cost: $2,500/attendee (annual, required for GM and Dir of Sales)

state_addenda_overrides.note: Structured overrides extracted in A2 Depth Pass 4

California.override: May be unenforceable under CA Civil Code §1671
CA B&P Code §§20000–20043 provides additional protections
NY choice of law may not be enforceable
VA venue may not be enforceable
Excludes CA Franchise Investment Law claims

Hawaii.override: Does not relieve from HI franchise law liability

Illinois.override: IL FDD Act governs for IL licensees; venue/jury waiver do not waive IL rights

Maryland.override: MD law may supersede FA on termination/renewal
May sue in MD for MD Franchise Law claims (3-year SOL)
No waiver of claims or disclaimer of reliance

Michigan.override: Out-of-state litigation may not be required
Cannot require release of MI FIL rights

Minnesota.override: Cannot require out-of-state litigation or jury waiver
Excludes MN Franchise Investment Law claims

New_York.override: Franchisee may terminate on any grounds available by law
NY Franchises Law governs NY causes of action
May not be enforceable under NY law

North_Dakota.override: ND law supersedes NY law completely
Excludes ND Franchise Disclosure Act claims

Rhode_Island.override: Excludes RI Franchise Investment Act claims
Venue/choice of law restrictions void for RI Act claims

Washington.override: WA Franchise Investment Protection Act prevails
Excludes WA Act rights except negotiated settlements with counsel
Arbitration/mediation must be in WA; may bring action in WA
Void unless employee >$100K or contractor >$250K annual earnings

unresolveds.description: No renewal right with $50M–$204M investment — franchise is non-renewable, re-licensing at franchisor's sole discretion with potentially materially different terms
All assets and franchise contracts pledged as collateral for Hilton parent debt obligations (maturing 2025–2033)
5 pending antitrust class actions related to IDeaS/STR/Amadeus revenue management software — uncertain outcome
Gag clause: some current and former franchisees restricted from speaking about their experience
No protected territory by default; negotiable Restricted Area has extensive carve-outs and time-limited Restrictive Period
No cost/profitability data in Item 19 — only top-line performance metrics (room rate, occupancy, indices)
Liquidated damages formula for pre-2yr termination uses system-wide average which may exceed hotel-specific average
Subsequent events note references March 18, 2024 but audit opinion dated March 18, 2025 — likely typographical error
